findings.message: DomSanitizer bypass with dynamic content can lead to XSS if the value is user-controlled.
findings.why: bypassSecurityTrust* tells Angular to skip sanitization; attacker-controlled strings become executable in the DOM.
findings.fix: Do not pass user input into bypass methods; sanitize with a trusted library or avoid HTML injection.
findings.confidenceReason: Pattern rule injection.xss.angular-sanitizer-bypass: bypassSecurityTrustHtml / Script / Style / Url / ResourceUrl with non-literal arguments.
confidenceReasons: Pattern rule injection.xss.angular-sanitizer-bypass: bypassSecurityTrustHtml / Script / Style / Url / ResourceUrl with non-literal arguments.
findings.proofReason: No proof pipeline output for this finding (detection-only).

fixPreview.remediation_hint: Do not pass user input into bypass methods; sanitize with a trusted library or avoid HTML injection.
fixPreview.expected_effect: Re-scan should clear the pattern if the vulnerability class is fully addressed; confirm with review.

ruleDocumentation.title: Angular DomSanitizer bypass with dynamic content
ruleDocumentation.pattern: bypassSecurityTrustHtml / Script / Style / Url / ResourceUrl with non-literal arguments.
ruleDocumentation.risk: Bypass methods disable Angular sanitization; XSS if the value is user-influenced.
ruleDocumentation.falsePositives: Hard-coded trusted snippets only—still prefer non-bypass APIs where possible.
ruleDocumentation.remediation: Never pass user input to bypassSecurityTrust*; sanitize and use safe bindings.
ruleDocumentation.secureExample: // Prefer bound text or DomSanitizer.sanitize with strict context

findings.message: No helmet() call detected in this file — verify security headers are set.
findings.why: Without secure headers, apps are more vulnerable to XSS, clickjacking, and MIME sniffing.
findings.fix: Use helmet() at app bootstrap or equivalent header middleware.
findings.confidenceReason: Pattern rule MW-003: App-level audit suspects missing helmet-style headers.
confidenceReasons: Pattern rule MW-003: App-level audit suspects missing helmet-style headers.
findings.proofReason: No proof pipeline output for this finding (detection-only).

fixPreview.remediation_hint: Use helmet() at app bootstrap or equivalent header middleware.
fixPreview.expected_effect: Re-scan should clear the pattern if the vulnerability class is fully addressed; confirm with review.

ruleDocumentation.title: Security header gap (heuristic)
ruleDocumentation.pattern: App-level audit suspects missing helmet-style headers.
ruleDocumentation.risk: Clickjacking, MIME sniffing, XSS without defense-in-depth.
ruleDocumentation.remediation: Align app + edge header policy; verify with integration tests.

findings.message: Redirect target may be controlled by the user (open redirect / phishing risk).
findings.why: Passing request data into redirect APIs (Express res.redirect, Next.js NextResponse.redirect / Response.redirect, etc.) without an allow-list lets attackers send victims to malicious sites.
findings.fix: Validate redirect targets against a fixed allow-list or use relative paths only. Reject absolute URLs from untrusted input.
findings.confidenceReason: Pattern rule injection.open-redirect: res.redirect (or similar) first argument is derived from req.query / req.body without validation.
confidenceReasons: Pattern rule injection.open-redirect: res.redirect (or similar) first argument is derived from req.query / req.body without validation.
findings.proofReason: No proof pipeline output for this finding (detection-only).

fixPreview.remediation_hint: Validate redirect targets against a fixed allow-list or use relative paths only. Reject absolute URLs from untrusted input.
fixPreview.expected_effect: Re-scan should clear the pattern if the vulnerability class is fully addressed; confirm with review.

ruleDocumentation.title: Open redirect
ruleDocumentation.pattern: res.redirect (or similar) first argument is derived from req.query / req.body without validation.
ruleDocumentation.risk: Attackers craft links that send users to phishing or malware sites while appearing to originate from your domain.
ruleDocumentation.falsePositives: Redirects built only from server-side allow lists.
ruleDocumentation.remediation: Allow-list redirect targets or use relative paths only; reject absolute URLs from untrusted input.

ruleDocumentation.pattern: App-level audit suspects missing helmet-style headers.

findings.message: SQL built with string concatenation or template literals can lead to SQL injection.
findings.why: Untrusted input concatenated into a SQL string can break out of the query and execute arbitrary SQL.
findings.fix: Use parameterized queries, e.g. db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]), or an ORM with parameter binding.
findings.confidenceReason: Pattern rule injection.sql.string-concat: Query strings assembled with user-controlled fragments.
confidenceReasons: Pattern rule injection.sql.string-concat: Query strings assembled with user-controlled fragments.
findings.proofReason: No proof pipeline output for this finding (detection-only).

fixPreview.remediation_hint: Use parameterized queries, e.g. db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]), or an ORM with parameter binding.
fixPreview.expected_effect: Re-scan should clear the pattern if the vulnerability class is fully addressed; confirm with review.

ruleDocumentation.title: SQL built via string concatenation
ruleDocumentation.pattern: Query strings assembled with user-controlled fragments.
ruleDocumentation.risk: Classic SQL injection leading to data exfiltration or destruction.
ruleDocumentation.falsePositives: Constants only; ensure no dynamic segments from request data.

findings.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML with non-literal HTML can cause XSS if the string is user-influenced.
findings.why: React skips escaping for __html; any attacker-controlled markup becomes executable in the page context.
findings.fix: Avoid raw HTML; use sanitized markup from a trusted pipeline or framework-safe components.
findings.confidenceReason: Pattern rule injection.xss.react-dangerously-set-inner-html: dangerouslySetInnerHTML / __html with a non-literal expression (including JSX attributes).
confidenceReasons: Pattern rule injection.xss.react-dangerously-set-inner-html: dangerouslySetInnerHTML / __html with a non-literal expression (including JSX attributes).
findings.proofReason: No proof pipeline output for this finding (detection-only).

fixPreview.remediation_hint: Avoid raw HTML; use sanitized markup from a trusted pipeline or framework-safe components.
fixPreview.expected_effect: Re-scan should clear the pattern if the vulnerability class is fully addressed; confirm with review.

ruleDocumentation.title: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML with dynamic HTML
ruleDocumentation.pattern: dangerouslySetInnerHTML / __html with a non-literal expression (including JSX attributes).
ruleDocumentation.risk: React does not escape __html; attacker-controlled strings become real DOM and scripts.
ruleDocumentation.falsePositives: Carefully sanitized HTML from a trusted server-side pipeline.
ruleDocumentation.remediation: Prefer plain text or framework components; sanitize HTML with a maintained library if you must render HTML.
